changelog:
v1.1.0 Changes:

• New: Hardware Upgrade Analyzer — detects your CPU socket, chipset, and DDR type, then shows actionable upgrade paths with best CPU for your platform, balanced GPU suggestions, and RAM guidance
• New: Platform upgrade recommendation — when your socket ceiling is low, suggests a complete AM5 build with 9800X3D (gaming) or 9950X3D (streaming) with reference specs and pricing
• New: CyberPowerPC links (US/UK) — non-affiliated, recommended from personal experience
• Improved: Hardware Rating now uses /10 bars instead of stars — more granular, matches FAST Score aesthetic
• Improved: GPU tiers recalibrated — 4080 SUPER moved to tier 8, 9900K/KF moved to tier 6
• Improved: Star-to-bar colour gradient — red (1) → orange → amber → green → cyan (10)
• Improved: GPU upgrade suggestions are now practical — balanced to CPU capability, won't suggest a 5090 for a 6/10 CPU
• Fixed: CPU benchmark dead-code elimination — NoOptimization attribute prevents JIT from inflating scores
• Fixed: GPU TDR crash on older cards — added 2-second wake-up phase before heavy rendering
• Fixed: Benchmark score calibration — GPU reference matched to 4080 SUPER, GPU sub-weights 80/20 FPS/variance
• Fixed: Importance labels — GPU now MED for CPU-bound, Memory MED and Storage LOW for GPU-bound
